rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JASON ROUSSOS' Motion To File Second Amended Complaint Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is GRANTED. The case regards chaotic bar fights in May 2016. Plaintiff says he recently learned new facts in discovery and wants to plead them. He does not seek to change the causes of action already pled. California has "a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments." (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) The trial date is continued to March 4, 2019; all statutory deadlines will run from that date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: KINSON WONG ET AL VS. BILL LEUNG, LEW, FONG, LEUNG, LEE & CHAN A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kinson Wong To Respond To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Kinson Wong And His Attorneys Archer Norris
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT BILL LEUNG, LEW, FONG, LEUNG, LEE & CHAN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kinson Wong To Respond To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Kinson Wong And His Attorneys Archer Norris Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED, no opposition filed. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,035.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: POLYMATHIC PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL VS. DEAN LUCA KRAUSE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Polymathic Properties, Inc., And Meyer Corporation, U.S.' Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint (Cont From 9/27/18)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 15, Plaintiffs Polymathic Properties, Inc., And Meyer Corporation, U.S.' Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint (Cont From 9/27/18) On September 12, 2018, the parties stipulated to allow plaintiffs to file a third amended complaint and a second amended answer to the cross-complaint in this action. The motions to permit those amended pleadings are GRANTED. From review of the new pleadings, I note that they appear to be a wholesale rewrite of the prior pleadings. I also note defendant's statement that, while stipulating to filing, he does "not agree that any of the claims asserted in these documents are properly pleaded" or "timely." This strongly suggests that motions on the pleadings are in the offing. I therefore STAY all other pending motions - including all discovery motions and the motion to bifurcate - until the pleadings are settled and the case's scope can be ascertained. Any party may then move to lift the stay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: BROADBAND ITV, INC. VS. OPENTV, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Broadband Itv,Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Request For Production, (2) Form Interrogatories General, (3) And Request For Admission To Defendant Opentv, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 8, Plaintiff Broadband Itv, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Request For Production, (2) Form Interrogatories General, (3) And Request For Admission To Defendant Opentv, Inc. I rule on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to discovery as follows: The "report and recommendation" dated September 20, 2018 is adopted and incorporated herein by reference. Requests for Production of Documents Documents must be in the possession, custody or control of defendant in order to be discoverable from it. I.e., defendant is not required to produce documents maintained by affiliates. Defendant is not required to produce privileged documents. 1, 3, 5, 26, 44: MOOT. Defendant has agreed to produce responsive documents after entry of a protective order. 6, 14-22, 28-35, 38-40, 42-43, 50, 52-55, 65-73: GRANTED IN PART. Defendant is to produce documents as set out in the "report and recommendation" dated September 20, 2018. Requests for Admission 1-6, 8: DENIED. Defendant's responses are adequate. 7, 9-12, 15-17: GRANTED IN PART, as outlined in the "report and recommendation" dated September 20, 2018. Interrogatories 15.1, 17.1: MOOT. Defendant has agreed to supplement its responses. 50.5, 50.6: DENIED. Sanctions No sanctions are awarded to either party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Broadband is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Stanford-Startx Fund, Llc'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 14, Plaintiff Stanford-Startx Fund, Llc'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment Plaintiffs' application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Any attachment amount may be subject to reduction based on a cross-complaint the parties are currently disputing. (See CCP §483.015(b)(2).) Any exigency argument by plaintiffs is undercut because this lawsuit was filed more than six months ago, based on facts plaintiffs say they have known for at least nine months. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY MURRY VS. JOHN T. HENDRICKS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT GEOFFREY MURRY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant Geoffrey Murry demurs to causes of action in the first amended cross-complaint (FACC). I rule as follows: 1st (Breach of Contract: Of Counsel Agreement). SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The FACC pleads that the parties operated under this agreement "until January 2014." (Id. at 3:26.) The first cross-complaint was not filed until July 2018, seemingly outside the four-year statute of limitations. (See CCP §337.) 2nd (Breach of Contract: Stock Purchase/Confidentiality Agreements). OVERRULED. This cause of action is adequately pled. Murry argues for a level of pleading particularity not required by California law. (See, e.g., Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) 3rd (Breach of Oral Contract): OVERRULED. Murry again argues for a level of pleading particularity that is not required, and causes of action may be pled in the alternative. (Burks v. Poppy Const. Co. (1962) 57 Cal.2d 463, 470.) 4th (Breach of Implied-In-Fact Contract): SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The FACC does not adequately plead this cause in the alternative. (See Klein v. Chevron USA (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1388-89.) 5th (Breach of Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing). OVERRULED. Again, Murry argues for a level of pleading particularity that is not required. 6th (Breach of Fiduciary Duty). OVERRULED. The FACC's allegations regarding the parties' relationships are adequate for the pleading stage. (See, e.g., FACC 9-11, 50.) 9th & 10th (Fraud, Negligent Misrepresentation). OVERRULED. These causes are pled with adequate particularity. (See, e.g., FACC 76, 82.) 11th (Business & Professions Code §17200). OVERRULED. This cause is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Murry is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: EQUITAS BIO/PHARMA SOLUTIONS , LLC. VS. HISCOX INSURANCE COMPANY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 28, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF EQUITAS BIO/PHARMA SOLUTIONS, LLC.'s Motion To Seal Complaint Plaintiff Equitas' motion to seal is GRANTED. The court concludes that the motion complies with the requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Equitas is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
